growth_records.medium: modified SME medium supplemented with nitrate (H2/CO2 feed)

up.method: GC (TCD/FID) mass balance of dissolved H2 in feed vs chemostat outflow, at maximum growth stage of HPC (20 MPa) culture; H2 is growth-limiting substrate
measured carbon fixation rate during optimal growth at 20 MPa (HPC), Figure 7B

up.units: mmol (rate not quantified per-condition in text)
up.method: ion chromatography of feed vs outflow; nitrate sole electron acceptor, respiratory nitrate ammonification

sec.method: alkaline hypochlorite/phenol nitroprusside spectrophotometric assay; product of nitrate ammonification

yields.Ks_H2_20MPa: 1.5-2.5 mM (Monod saturation constant for H2 at 20 MPa)

growth_records.cite: Smedile, Francesco et al. Adaptations to high pressure of Nautilia sp. strain PV-1, a piezophilic Campylobacterium (aka Epsilonproteobacterium) isolated from a deep-sea hydrothermal vent. Environmental Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.16256. [PMC10092268]
growth_records.method: reported (mu from slope of ln(cell density) vs time; at steady state mu=D=1.1 h-1 at 2 ml/min); maximum mu=2.6 h-1 (doubling ~16 min) and maximum sustained dilution rate 2.61 h-1 at 20 MPa
growth_records.snippet: The rates of H2 utilization at the maximum growth stages of the HPC and LPC cultures were 72 ± 12 and 11 ± 1.2 fmol cell−1 h−1 ... at 20 MPa ... doubling time of ~16 min and a growth rate of 2.6 h−1 ... maximum dilution rates ... 0.5 and 20
growth_records.notes: HPC = high-pressure chemostat, 20 MPa, 55 C. Continuous culture, mu=D at steady state. Reported mu=1.1 h-1 at flow 2 ml/min during proteomic sampling; doubling_time_h computed: td=ln2/mu=0.6931/1.1=0.63 h. Maximum mu=2.6 h-1 cross-checks with reported doubling ~16 min: td=16/60=0.2667 h, mu=ln2/0.2667=2.60 h-1 (agrees). Max sustained dilution 2.61 h-1. PRIORITY experimental uptake: H2 72±12 fmol/cell/h and CO2 fixation 306±91 fgC/cell/d (both measured, per-cell units as reported; not convertible to mmol/gDW/h without cell dry mass, not given). Full SME mineral composition referenced to Vetriani et al. 2004 / Grosche et al. 2015, not restated (medium_composition_stated=false). Ks(H2)=1.5-2.5 mM at 20 MPa.

growth_records.medium: modified SME medium supplemented with nitrate (H2/CO2 feed)

up.method: GC mass balance of dissolved H2 feed vs outflow, at maximum growth stage of LPC (0.5 MPa) culture
measured carbon fixation rate at 0.5 MPa (LPC) under same dilution rate as HPC, Figure 7B; ~1 order of magnitude lower than HPC

yields.Ks_H2_0.5MPa: <1 mM (Monod saturation constant for H2 at 0.5 MPa)

growth_records.cite: Smedile, Francesco et al. Adaptations to high pressure of Nautilia sp. strain PV-1, a piezophilic Campylobacterium (aka Epsilonproteobacterium) isolated from a deep-sea hydrothermal vent. Environmental Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.16256. [PMC10092268]
growth_records.method: reported (mu=D at steady state); text gives LPC growth rate of 1.1 or 1.6 h-1; maximum sustained dilution rate at 0.5 MPa = 1.60 h-1
growth_records.snippet: low pressure in the chemostat (LPC; time: 1460-1633 h) ... at 0.5 MPa ... a lower flow rate of 0.2 ml min−1 to avoid cell washout ... 11 ± 1.2 fmol cell−1 h−1 ... reached values of 37 ± 19 fgC cell−1 d−1
growth_records.notes: LPC = low-pressure chemostat, 0.5 MPa, 55 C, flow 0.2 ml/min. mu=D at steady state. Text states LPC growth rate '1.1 or 1.6 h-1'; adopted 1.1 h-1 (doubling_time_h=ln2/1.1=0.63 h). Max sustained dilution at 0.5 MPa = 1.60 h-1. PRIORITY experimental uptake: H2 11±1.2 fmol/cell/h and CO2 fixation 37±19 fgC/cell/d (measured, per-cell units). LPC H2-limited vs HPC. Full SME composition referenced, not restated. Ks(H2)<1 mM at 0.5 MPa.

growth_records.medium: modified SME medium supplemented with nitrate (H2/CO2 headspace)

up.method: H2 electron donor; qualitative (LPB nutrient-limited, lowest growth performance)
CO2 concentration decreases during batch growth; rTCA carbon fixation enzymes upregulated in LPB

growth_records.cite: Smedile, Francesco et al. Adaptations to high pressure of Nautilia sp. strain PV-1, a piezophilic Campylobacterium (aka Epsilonproteobacterium) isolated from a deep-sea hydrothermal vent. Environmental Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.16256. [PMC10092268]
growth_records.method: reported (doubling time 86 min, growth rate 0.48 h-1); consistent with mu=ln2/td
growth_records.snippet: The doubling time and growth rate of strain PV-1 when grown in batch were 86 min and 0.48 h−1, respectively, compared to a growth rate of 1.1 or 1.6 h−1 in the LPC regime, indicating that the growth performance of strain PV-1 was lowest in
growth_records.notes: LPB = low-pressure batch, 0.2 MPa, 55 C. Reported doubling 86 min = 1.433 h; growth rate 0.48 h-1. Cross-check: mu=ln2/td=0.6931/1.433=0.484 h-1 (agrees). No per-condition H2/CO2/nitrate uptake rates reported for LPB (rates given only for HPC and LPC chemostat conditions). Growth nutrient-limited/lowest of three regimes. Full SME composition referenced, not restated.
